leadText: The Silver Feather (Japanese: ぎんいろのはね Silver Feather), called Silver Wing prior to Pokémon Scarlet and Violet, is a Key Item introduced in Generation II. It is a legendary artifact that is associated with Lugia.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] The Silver Wing allows the player to a encounter a wild Lugia at Whirl Islands B2F G S C or B3F HG SS if the player has entered the cave through the northeastern island.

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text: Acquisition [ edit | edit source ] Games Method G C HG Pewter City (from an old man near the exit to Route 3 ) S SS Goldenrod Radio Tower (from the Director after defeating Team Rocket )

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description G S C A strange, silver-colored feather. HG SS B W B2 W2 X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA A strange, silvery feather that sparkles. S V A strange feather that gleams with a silvery color.

sections.title: In the core series games
sections.text: Contents 1 In the core series games 1.1 Effect 1.2 Generation II 1.3 Generation IV 1.4 Description 1.5 Acquisition In the core series games [ edit | edit source ] Effect [ edit | edit source ] The Silver Wing allows the player to a encounter a wild Lugia at Whirl Islands B2F G S C or B3F HG SS if the player has entered the cave through the northeastern island. Generation II [ edit | edit source ] In Generation II , although it is possible to store the Silver Wing in the PC , Lugia will only appear if the Silver Wing is currently in the Bag . If the player defeats or runs away from Lugia, it will be permanently unavailable in the current game. Generation IV [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon SoulSilver, both the Silver Wing and Tidal Bell are required to summon Lugia. In Pokémon HeartGold, only the Silver Wing is required. In both Pokémon HeartGold and SoulSilver, if the player defeats or runs away from Lugia, it will reappear the next time the player enters the Hall of Fame . Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description G S C A strange, silver-colored feather. HG SS B W B2 W2 X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA A strange, silvery feather that sparkles. S V A strange feather that gleams with a silvery color. Acquisition [ edit | edit source ] Games Method G C HG Pewter City (from an old man near the exit to Route 3 ) S SS Goldenrod Radio Tower (from the Director after defeating Team Rocket )
